summary: Incendiary Watchtower solo, monster Hell‑in‑a‑Bucket, strong Dylan set
blurb: The show delivers an incendiary All Along the Watchtower solo and a thunderous Hell In A Bucket. Brent’s lap‑steel adds fire to West L.A. Fadeaway while the Dylan segment shines with Slow Train and Joey. The Miller soundboard capture is clean enough for a solid listening experience.
review: On July 4, 1987 the Dead turned Sullivan Stadium into a summer fireworks display of rock and blues. Jerry Garcia roared through an incendiary All Along the Watchtower solo and set the stage ablaze with a monster Hell In A Bucket, while his occasional lap‑steel added a surprising texture. Brent Mydland’s keyboards were on fire too – his “Tons Of Steel” vibe lifted West L.A. Fadeaway into a high‑energy highlight, and he even contributed backing vocals at the start of the Dylan set. The second half featured the Dylan collaboration in full force: Slow Train Coming and Joey were performed with gusto, and Bob Weir’s vocal on Watchtower was singled out as a standout moment. Though Touch of Grey drew mixed reactions, the overall performance is tight, fast‑paced, and memorable, especially given the heat‑soaked stadium atmosphere. The recommended Miller SBD (gd1987-07-04.sbd.miller.101650.flac16) captures the band’s fire with a clean low end and minimal dropouts, making this show a solid entry for any Deadhead’s collection.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings use enthusiastic language ('incendiary', 'monster', 'standout') to describe Watchtower, Hell In A Bucket, Slow Train and Joey, with no recurring criticism of the band's playing. Two recordings are explicitly positive and two are mixed without strong negative remarks, yielding a modestly positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with clean mix, strong low end, and clear presentation of the highlighted Watchtower solo and Hell In A Bucket performance.

key_highlights: Incendiary All Along The Watchtower solo
Strong Dylan collaboration featuring Slow Train and Joey
Brent’s lap‑steel fire on West L.A. Fadeaway

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring solos on Watchtower, fiery lead on Hell In A Bucket, and adds rare lap‑steel moments that punctuate the set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocal on All Along The Watchtower is highlighted as a standout contribution.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland shines with lap‑steel on West L.A. Fadeaway, provides ‘Tons Of Steel’ texture and adds backing vocals at the start of the Dylan set.
